reconstruction_test: If 2.33 were removed from the chapter, could the engineering analog be reconstructed from 2.32 (unsought-arrival) plus 2.34 (imperishable-disrepute)?
result: No — 2.32 names the moment of arrival and 2.34 names the consequence of withdrawal in reputational terms, but neither names the if-then conditional structure that 2.33 distinctly carries (atha cet... tataḥ). The three-fold loss specification (svadharma, kīrti, pāpa) is also unique to 2.33; 2.34 picks up only the kīrti-loss to develop. The mid-incident-walking-out scenario specifically requires the if-then form to land — without 2.33's atha cet, the scenario would be either an unsought-arrival case (2.32) or an imperishable-disrepute case (2.34), but not the conditional loss-trio.
verdict_reason: The engineering layer carries 2.33-specific doctrinal load (if-then form, three-fold loss) that cannot be reconstructed from neighbours. PASS.
